prompt: At the two-minute mark in the YouTube video uploaded by the channel “GameGrumps” on May 14, 2017 as part of their playthrough of the game Mario Kart 8 Deluxe, the shows’ hosts are competing on one of the game’s racetracks. What was the world record time for that track in the game’s 150cc mode as of June 7, 2023? Express your answer in minutes and seconds, rounding the seconds to the nearest hundredth, e.g. 1:01.001.
actual:
1:41.614

The GameGrumps video uploaded on May 14, 2017 is "Mario Kart 8 Deluxe: The Grand Prix - PART 7 - Game Grumps VS" (video ID: nvaLkvUkW0w). Based on YouTube comments confirming the Egg Cup tracks (Dragon Driftway at 7:20-8:49, Excitebike Arena mentioned with ramps, and the "last race" comeback at 9:33-11:03), at the two-minute mark they were racing on **GCN Yoshi Circuit**, the first track of the Egg Cup.

According to mkwrs.com (the Mario Kart 8 Deluxe world records tracker), the 150cc world record for GCN Yoshi Circuit active on June 7, 2023 was **1'41"614** (1 minute, 41.614 seconds), set by player Army on March 10, 2023. This record stood until June 15, 2023, when it was broken by stove with a time of 1'41"603.
finish_summary:
1:41.614

According to mkwrs.com (the Mario Kart 8 Deluxe world records tracker), the 150cc world record for GCN Yoshi Circuit active on June 7, 2023 was **1'41"614** (1 minute, 41.614 seconds), set by player Army on March 10, 2023. This record stood until June 15, 2023, when it was broken by stove with a time of 1'41"603.
